title: Regression?: About podman umount behavior
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

1. podman umount allows unmounting running containers.

I don't know which behavior is correct.

<details>
<summary>podman v1.6.2</summary>

```
# podman run -d --name running quay.io/libpod/testimage:20220615 top
c6bc921eaac0c95123dc428d698460d67ee14a9f1e2216cc2d10a7a49999d5d4

# podman mount
c6bc921eaac0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/f3275ea34bf3006cf819f1050f7645682e0c2c03db6aa262050b4a30bc6e6fee/merged

# podman umount running

# podman mount
c6bc921eaac0 /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/f3275ea34bf3006cf819f1050f7645682e0c2c03db6aa262050b4a30bc6e6fee/merged
```

</details>


<details>
<summary>podman v4.2.0 dev</summary>

```
# podman run -d --name running quay.io/libpod/testimage:20220615 top
b3d5bf4f41a1b9429af92f8629540b891913553aa27c3d67dd82f47681852036

# podman mount
b3d5bf4f41a1  /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/5d7d842edaf1c4a421ac5563a287440fff89facb723d6847748664c2f79066e9/merged

# podman umount running
running

# podman mount
```

</details>

2. podman umount doesn't output error messages even if the storage container isn't mounted.

I think error messages should be outputted like older version podman.

<details>
<summary>podman v1.6.2</summary>

```
# podman run -d --name stopped quay.io/libpod/testimage:20220615 top
afbef612d0e81d8bf5f436e8e223466b286a973a6deb6d4f49d640ea85336312

# podman stop stopped
afbef612d0e81d8bf5f436e8e223466b286a973a6deb6d4f49d640ea85336312

# podman mount

# podman umount stopped
Error: error unmounting continaner afbef612d0e81d8bf5f436e8e223466b286a973a6deb6d4f49d640ea85336312: error unmounting container afbef612d0e81d8bf5f436e8e223466b286a973a6deb6d4f49d640ea85336312 root filesystem: layer is not mounted
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>podman v4.2.0 dev</summary>

```
# podman run -d --name stopped quay.io/libpod/testimage:20220615 top
29377846b6cf34949a3baa411c05ab571397850dcf5e07af06cbaa0238396756

# podman stop stopped
stopped

# podman mount

# podman umount stopped
stopped
```

</details>

May be related to #8185

---

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
KVM

comments.body: I sort of remember this being a conscious decision. Perhaps because we could not be sure of how many times an image was mounted.  @nalind Do you recall?
This is definitely a bug IMO. I recall us wiring safeguards into the mount/unmount code to prevent this when it was first written?
We count the number of mounts and unmounts, and when it gets to zero we unmount.

I remember code to prevent the counter from going under 1 so long as the container was running (we have a bool in the state tracking whether the container is running, and if it was set we would not let the counter decrement to 0).

The command is not unmounting the image from under a running container.  

The command is reporting success, and removing the mount from the database, but the container is still running with the mount inside of its mount namespace.

As far as the current mount namespace is concerned the container is unmounted.
Ahhhh. Good point. Working as advertised, then. I need more coffee.
@rhatdan Should we close the issue if it is working as expected.
